info.name: 04-FailureAndRetry
info.description: Resolution axis: a refund that FAILS at the Stripe call goes FAILED, voids its provisional leg (no orphaned money), and recovers on retry (approve+retryRefunds) to REFUNDED. Owns the FAILED->retry state machine + #19 (refunds=REFUND_FAILURE + refund_request.refunds_id link). (The #37 double-charge guard needs a reliably-orchestrated FAILED-with-re_ state, which the poll harness can't produce deterministically — deferred.)

exec: // Dynamic refund amounts derived from the real ride's charged amount T (self-captured in THIS run's gate, NOT carried from 00).
// rf1 = 0.5*cap (primary), rf2 = 0.25*cap (secondary partial), rf3 = 0.5*cap. Sized off the REAL RIDE_FARE
// component cap (rideFareCap, captured from fare_breakup_info) — NOT off T — so rf1 never spuriously trips
// the per-component cap on a ride with fees/toll/parking. Falls back to T until the gate sets rideFareCap.

item.name: Rider Auth
Rider OTP
Gate: latest COMPLETED booking for fixed rider (run 00 first)

exec: var b = completed.length ? completed[0] : (list.length ? list[0] : null);

pm.expect(b != null, 'no completed booking for the fixed rider; run 00 (FixtureStripeRide) first').to.eql(true);
if (b) { pm.environment.set('customer_bookingId', b.id); console.log('Gate: booking ' + b.id + ' status ' + b.status); }
var d = {}; try { d = pm.response.json(); } catch(e) {}
// the list handler can 500 transiently on an unrelated async notification fork — retry once before failing
pm.sendRequest({url: pm.request.url.toString(), method: 'GET', header: {token: pm.environment.get('rider_token')}}, function (err, res) {
var d2 = {}; try { d2 = res.json(); } catch(e) {}

item.name: Capture BAP ride id (SQL — poll until drained)

exec: if(!rows.length){ pm.test('BAP ride row drained (run 00 first)', function(){ pm.expect(0,'no ride for booking; run 00').to.be.above(0); }); return; }
// NOTE: r.id is the RIDE id. orderId is a SEPARATE payment_order GUID, captured next via domain_entity_id.

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{ mode:'raw', raw:_sqlBody() } }, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=((res&&res.json())||{}).rows||[]; }catch(e){} if(rows.length){ _finish(rows); } else if(n>0){ setTimeout(function(){ _poll(n-1); }, 3000); } else { _finish([]); } }); }

item.name: Capture payment order short id + intent (SQL — poll until drained)

exec: if(!rows.length){ pm.test('payment_order row present', function(){ pm.expect(0,'no payment_order for ride').to.be.above(0); }); return; }

pm.test('payment_order row present (orderId != rideId)', function(){ pm.expect(o.id).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.expect(o.id, 'orderId must be a real GUID, not the rideId').to.not.eql(pm.environment.get('rideId')); });
pm.test('order has a Stripe payment intent (pi_)', function(){ pm.expect(/^pi_/.test(String(o.payment_service_order_id))).to.eql(true); });

item.name: Capture RIDE_FARE component cap (SQL fare_breakup_info — refund cap source)

exec: // RIDE_FARE cap = sum of these tags (= li.rideFare + li.gstAmount, RidePayment.hs:841). TOLL/PARKING analogous (D1 uses tollCap/parkingCap).
function _sum(arr, tags){ return (arr||[]).filter(function(b){ return tags.indexOf(b.description) >= 0; }).reduce(function(a,b){ return a + Number(b.amount||0); }, 0); }
if (rows && rows.length) { try { bk = JSON.parse(rows[0].fare_breakups || '[]'); } catch(e) { bk = []; } }
console.log('Gate: rideFareCap='+rfCap+' tollCap='+tollCap+' parkingCap='+parkCap+' (from fare_breakup_info; refund amounts fall back to T if rideFareCap=0)');
pm.test('RIDE_FARE component cap captured from fare_breakup_info', function(){ pm.expect(rfCap, 'zero RIDE_FARE cap — stale fixture, re-run 00').to.be.above(0); });

// Refund amounts sized off the REAL RIDE_FARE component cap (rideFareCap from fare_breakup_info),

item.name: Reset refund state for the ride (clean start)

exec: // SYNCHRONOUS single clear — the runner waits for THIS request, so no async re-fire can wipe a later-created refund_request.
pm.test('refund state reset (synchronous clean start)', function(){ pm.expect(pm.response.code, pm.response.text()).to.be.oneOf([200,201]); });

item.name: Clear mock overrides (avoid leaked async state from an interrupted prior run)

exec: pm.test('mock overrides cleared (clean slate)', function(){ pm.expect(pm.response.code, pm.response.text()).to.be.oneOf([200,201,204]); });

item.name: Switch City for BAP (Rider Dashboard)

item.name: Raise Refund rf2 = T/4 (Customer)
Mock override: refund create -> error (no re_)
Capture refundRequestId (SQL — after drain)
Approve -> FAILED (no re_)
Assert FAILED state: request FAILED + refunds REFUND_FAILURE + refunds_id link (#19)

exec: pm.test('#19 refund_request reached FAILED', function(){ pm.expect(rr.length).to.be.above(0); pm.expect(rr[0].status).to.eql('FAILED'); });
// nested: assert the linked refunds row is REFUND_FAILURE and refunds_id points at the internal Refunds id
if(refs.filter(function(x){return x.status==='REFUND_FAILURE';}).length===0 && k>0){ setTimeout(function(){ _refPoll(k-1); }, 3000); return; } // KV->PG drain: wait for the refunds row

var _fail=refs.filter(function(x){return x.status==='REFUND_FAILURE';}); pm.test('#19 a REFUND_FAILURE refunds row exists (no re_)', function(){ pm.expect(_fail.length, '#19 REFUND_FAILURE refunds row must exist').to.be.above(0); pm.expect(_fail[0].id_assigned_by_service_provider==null||_fail[0].id_assigned_by_service_provider==='', 'create-error assigns no re_').to.be.true; });
pm.test('#19 refund_request.refunds_id links the internal REFUND_FAILURE Refunds row (not the Stripe re_)', function(){
pm.expect(_fail.length, '#19 REFUND_FAILURE refunds row must exist (link target)').to.be.above(0);
pm.expect(rid, 'refunds_id must equal the REFUND_FAILURE refunds row id').to.eql(_fail[0].id);

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select',method:'POST',header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'},body:{mode:'raw',raw:_rrBody()}}, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=((res&&res.json())||{}).rows||[]; }catch(e){} if(_ok(rows)||n<=0){ _assert(rows); } else { setTimeout(function(){ _poll(n-1); }, 3000); } }); }

item.name: Retry without retryRefunds flag -> 400

exec: pm.test('re-approve of a FAILED request WITHOUT retryRefunds is rejected with 400 mentioning retryRefund', function () {
pm.expect(pm.response.text(), 'error should point the admin at the retryRefunds flag').to.match(/retryRefund/i);

item.name: Clear mock override
Retry approve -> REFUNDED (recovers)
Assert failed attempt VOIDED its leg + retry recovered SETTLED (no orphan money)

exec: function _ok(rows){ var rr=rows.filter(_isRefund); return rr.some(function(r){return r.status==='VOIDED';}) && rr.some(function(r){return r.status==='SETTLED';}); }
// rf2's FIRST (create-error) attempt posts PENDING legs then VOIDs them; the retry posts fresh legs that SETTLE.
try { console.log('  ↳ BAP rf2 refund ledger:', JSON.stringify((rows||[]).filter(_isRefund).map(function(x){return {st:x.status, amt:x.amount, type:x.reference_type};}))); } catch(e) {}
var voided=rr.filter(function(r){return r.status==='VOIDED';}); var settled=rr.filter(function(r){return r.status==='SETTLED';});
pm.test('Failed attempt VOIDED >=1 provisional leg (no orphaned money)', function(){ pm.expect(voided.length,'expected >=1 VOIDED leg from the create-error attempt').to.be.above(0); });

pm.test('Retry recovered: >=1 SETTLED leg summing to rf2', function(){ pm.expect(settled.length,'expected >=1 SETTLED leg after retry').to.be.above(0); var s=settled.reduce(function(a,r){return a+Number(r.amount);},0); pm.expect(s).to.be.closeTo(Number(pm.environment.get('rf2')),0.02); });

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{mode:'raw', raw:_sqlBody()} }, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=((res&&res.json())||{}).rows||[]; }catch(e){} if(_ok(rows) || n<=0){ _assert(rows); } else { setTimeout(function(){ _poll(n-1); }, 3000); } }); }
function _ok(rows){ var rr=rows.filter(_isRefund); return rr.some(function(r){return r.status==='VOIDED';}) && rr.filter(function(r){return r.status==='SETTLED';}).length>=2; }
// create-error attempt: provisional refund legs VOIDED; the rf2 retry posts fresh per-component legs that SETTLE (summing to rf2).
try { console.log('  ↳ BAP ride refund ledger:', JSON.stringify((rows||[]).filter(_isRefund).map(function(x){return {st:x.status, amt:x.amount, type:x.reference_type};}))); } catch(e) {}
pm.test('BAP refund ledger: >=1 VOIDED (failed no-re_ attempt) and >=2 SETTLED (rf2 retry per-component legs)', function () {
pm.expect(voided.length, 'expected >=1 VOIDED refund leg from the no-re_ failed refund').to.be.above(0);
pm.expect(settled.length, 'expected >=2 SETTLED refund legs (rf2 retry per-component legs)').to.be.above(1);

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{mode:'raw', raw:_sqlBody()} }, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=((res&&res.json())||{}).rows||[]; }catch(e){} if(_ok(rows) || n<=0){ _assert(rows); } else { setTimeout(function(){ _poll(n-1); }, 3000); } }); }
